### command\_btle\_test\_le\_tx\_start<!-- {{#callable_declaration:command_btle_test_le_tx_start}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/prompt_commands.h#L281>)

Starts a Bluetooth LE transmitter test.
- **Description**: This function initiates a Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) transmitter test. It must be called with valid parameters representing the transmission channel, packet length, and payload type. The parameters should be provided as strings that can be converted to integers. If any parameter is invalid or cannot be converted, the behavior is undefined.
- **Inputs**:
    - `tx_channel`: A string representing the transmission channel. It must be convertible to an integer. Must not be null.
    - `tx_packet_length`: A string representing the length of the packets to be transmitted. It must be convertible to an integer. Must not be null.
    - `packet_payload_type`: A string representing the type of payload for the packets. It must be convertible to an integer. Must not be null.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`command_btle_test_le_tx_start`](<prompt_commands.c.md#command_btle_test_le_tx_start>)  (Implementation)

### command\_btle\_test\_rx\_start<!-- {{#callable_declaration:command_btle_test_rx_start}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/prompt_commands.h#L283>)

Starts receiving Bluetooth Low Energy test packets.
- **Description**: This function initiates the reception of Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) test packets on a specified channel. It should be called when the system is ready to perform BLE testing. The input parameter must represent a valid channel number. If the input is invalid, the behavior is undefined.
- **Inputs**:
    - `rx_channel`: A string representing the channel number for receiving BLE packets. It must be a valid integer string. The caller retains ownership of the string. If the string cannot be converted to an integer, the function may not behave as expected.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`command_btle_test_rx_start`](<prompt_commands.c.md#command_btle_test_rx_start>)  (Implementation)

Ends the Bluetooth Low Energy test.
- **Description**: This function is used to terminate an ongoing Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) test. It should be called when the BLE test is no longer needed, ensuring that any resources allocated for the test are properly released. There are no specific preconditions for calling this function, but it is expected to be used in the context of BLE testing.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`command_btle_test_end`](<prompt_commands.c.md#command_btle_test_end>)  (Implementation)

### command\_btle\_pa\_set<!-- {{#callable_declaration:command_btle_pa_set}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/prompt_commands.h#L285>)

Sets the Bluetooth Low Energy power amplifier option.
- **Description**: This function configures the Bluetooth Low Energy (BTLE) power amplifier based on the provided command string. It should be called with a valid option string that matches one of the predefined configurations. If the command is valid, it sets the power amplifier to the specified option. If the command is invalid, it sends a response listing the available options. Ensure that the command string is not null and is properly formatted.
- **Inputs**:
    - `command`: A pointer to a string representing the desired power amplifier option. Must not be null and should match one of the predefined options. If the command does not match any valid options, a response will indicate the available options.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`command_btle_pa_set`](<prompt_commands.c.md#command_btle_pa_set>)  (Implementation)

### command\_btle\_unmod\_tx\_start<!-- {{#callable_declaration:command_btle_unmod_tx_start}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/prompt_commands.h#L286>)

Starts unmodulated transmission on a specified Bluetooth channel.
- **Description**: This function initiates unmodulated transmission over Bluetooth using the specified channel. It is important to call this function with a valid channel string that can be converted to an integer. The function expects the channel to be a numeric string, and it will convert this string to an integer using `atoi`. If the conversion results in an invalid channel, the behavior is undefined. Ensure that the Bluetooth subsystem is properly initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `tx_channel`: A string representing the transmission channel. It must be a valid numeric string that can be converted to an integer. The caller retains ownership of the string. If the string cannot be converted to a valid integer, the behavior is undefined.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`command_btle_unmod_tx_start`](<prompt_commands.c.md#command_btle_unmod_tx_start>)  (Implementation)

Stops unmodulated Bluetooth transmission.
- **Description**: This function is used to stop the unmodulated Bluetooth transmission. It should be called when the transmission is no longer needed, such as when the application is shutting down or switching to a different mode. There are no specific preconditions for calling this function, and it does not require any parameters.

- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`command_btle_unmod_tx_stop`](<prompt_commands.c.md#command_btle_unmod_tx_stop>)  (Implementation)

### command\_hrm\_read<!-- {{#callable_declaration:command_hrm_read}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/prompt_commands.h#L290>)

Reads heart rate monitor data.
- **Description**: This function subscribes to the heart rate monitor service and begins reading data. It must be called when the system is ready to handle heart rate data. The function unsubscribes from any previous heart rate monitor session before starting a new one. It is important to ensure that the system is in a state that allows for heart rate monitoring.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`command_hrm_read`](<../services/common/hrm/hrm_manager.c.md#command_hrm_read>)  (Implementation)

### command\_hrm\_wipe<!-- {{#callable_declaration:command_hrm_wipe}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/prompt_commands.h#L291>)

Wipes the HRM firmware.
- **Description**: This function is used to invalidate the HRM firmware by writing specific values to the magic number region. It must be called when the system is in a state that allows firmware modification. The function locks the HRM state to ensure thread safety during the operation. It performs a series of write operations to the firmware, and if any of these operations fail, it will indicate an error. After the operation, it releases the lock and sends a response indicating whether the operation was successful.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns nothing and does not mutate any inputs.
- **See Also**: [`command_hrm_wipe`](<../drivers/hrm/as7000.c.md#command_hrm_wipe>)  (Implementation)

### command\_hrm\_freeze<!-- {{#callable_declaration:command_hrm_freeze}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/prompt_commands.h#L292>)

Freezes the heart rate monitor.
- **Description**: This function is used to disable the heart rate monitor when it is currently enabled. It should be called when the heart rate monitor needs to be temporarily paused, such as during a system reset or when the device is entering a low-power state. The function ensures that the necessary hardware components are properly disabled to prevent any unintended operation. It is important to ensure that the heart rate monitor is enabled before calling this function; otherwise, it will have no effect.
- **Inputs**:
    - `None`: This function does not take any parameters.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`command_hrm_freeze`](<../drivers/hrm/as7000.c.md#command_hrm_freeze>)  (Implementation)

### command\_perftest\_line<!-- {{#callable_declaration:command_perftest_line}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/prompt_commands.h#L299>)

Executes a performance test by drawing lines on the display.
- **Description**: This function is used to perform a graphical performance test by drawing multiple lines on the display. It requires two string parameters: one to specify whether antialiasing is enabled or not, and another to set the stroke width for the lines. The first parameter must be either 'aa' to enable antialiasing or 'noaa' to disable it; any other value will result in an error message being sent. The second parameter should represent a valid integer for the stroke width. The function will send a response with the results of the performance test, including the time taken to draw the lines.
- **Inputs**:
    - `do_aa`: Specifies whether to enable antialiasing. Must be 'aa' or 'noaa'. Any other value will trigger an error response.
    - `width`: Sets the stroke width for the lines. Must be a valid integer string. The function will convert this string to an integer.
- **Output**: Returns nothing and sends a formatted response with the performance test results.
- **See Also**: [`command_perftest_line`](<prompt_commands.c.md#command_perftest_line>)  (Implementation)

[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/prompt_commands.h#L300>)

Executes a series of performance tests.
- **Description**: This function is used to run a predefined set of performance tests. It sends a prompt to the user indicating the parameters being tested, including antialiasing, width, total time, and total cycles. It is important to call this function in an appropriate context where performance testing is required. There are no parameters to pass, and it does not return any values.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`command_perftest_line_all`](<prompt_commands.c.md#command_perftest_line_all>)  (Implementation)

### command\_perftest\_text<!-- {{#callable_declaration:command_perftest_text}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/prompt_commands.h#L301>)

Executes a performance test with specified text parameters.
- **Description**: This function is used to initiate a performance test that displays text based on the provided parameters. It should be called when the system is ready to perform a performance test. The function sets the string type, font key, and vertical offset for the text display. It will continue to run until the string type is set to `NULL`, allowing for the performance test to be monitored and managed during its execution.
- **Inputs**:
    - `string_type`: Specifies the type of string to display. Must not be null.
    - `fontkey`: Identifies the font to use for the text display. Must not be null.
    - `yoffset`: Defines the vertical offset for the text display. Must not be null.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`command_perftest_text`](<prompt_commands.c.md#command_perftest_text>)  (Implementation)

Executes performance tests for various text configurations.
- **Description**: This function is used to run a series of performance tests on different text configurations, including various fonts, types, and offsets. It sends a prompt response indicating the parameters being tested and iterates through predefined combinations of fonts, types, and offsets to execute the performance test. It is important to call this function in an appropriate context where performance testing is required. There are no parameters to pass, and it does not return any value.
- **Inputs**:
    - `None`: This function does not take any input parameters.
- **Output**: None

### command\_bt\_sleep\_check<!-- {{#callable_declaration:command_bt_sleep_check}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/prompt_commands.h#L304>)

Checks Bluetooth sleep mode.
- **Description**: This function initiates a Bluetooth sleep check test. It must be called when the Bluetooth chip is not in test mode. If the chip is in test mode, an error message is sent, and the function returns without starting the test. If the function is called successfully, it creates a timer for the sleep check and sends a prompt indicating that the test has started. The caller should ensure that the Bluetooth system is properly initialized before invoking this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `iters`: A string representing the number of iterations for the sleep check. It must not be null. If the string cannot be converted to an integer, the behavior is undefined.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`command_bt_sleep_check`](<prompt_commands.c.md#command_bt_sleep_check>)  (Implementation)
